publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-08-13 00:13:32 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Professional papers and an extensive photograph collection pertaining to Professor Pelzer's work in Southeast Asia, primarily Indonesia and the Philippines.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Karl J. Pelzer Papers, #4267. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Karl Pelzer was born in Oberpleis, Germany, and graduated from the University of Bonn, receiving his doctorate there in 1935. After coming to the United States, he taught at the University of California at Berkeley and at Johns Hopkins University. During World War II he was with the Office of War Information.

From 1945-1947 he worked for the Department of Agriculture, in its office of foreign agricultural relations, and he was part of a unit that toured Japan, the Philippines and the East Indies to study the effects of war on agriculture.
He was a member of the Yale University faculty from 1947 until his retirement in 1977, and was for many years the director of Southeast Asia Studies.

Pelzer was an authority on land use and the demographics of tropical regions, particularly Southeast Asia. He was the author of Population and Land Utilization and Pioneer Settlement in the Asiatic Tropics.
He died at his home in North Haven, Connecticut at the age of 71.
From: "Karl J. Pelzer Is Dead; Professor Was Expert On Land Use in Asia" THE NEW YORK TIMES, November 12, 1980.

controlaccess.subject: Geography -- Study and teaching.
Agriculture -- Indonesia.

Forests and forestry -- Philippines.
Land reform--Philippines.
Mennonites.
Southeast Asia--Social life and customs--20th century.

World War, 1939-1945--Refugees.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 10 cm long; possibly a Betel nut.

"A Water Gate in Venice" etching by Joseph Pennell

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Bowman was an American geographer and President of the Johns Hopkins University, 1935–1948. He was controversial for his antisemitism and inaction in Jewish resettlement during WWII.

did.unittitle: Bowman, Isaiah -- letters about Pioneer Fringe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies of letters from Franklin D. Roosevelt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Alaska reports, including report on resettling refugees in the state.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Pelzer's FOIA request for information on German spy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Sikkim biweekly newspaper and Sri Lankan travel prospectuses. The latter refer to Sri Lanka as Ceylon, the name British Colonialists gave the island, which received its independence in 1948.

U.S. Office of War Information reports

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Unless otherwise noted, folder titles are the ones Pelzer gave to his original files. Occassionally he referred to geographic locations by names that are now no longer recognized, having usually been colonialist in origin. In these instances the native location names have been included by the archivist in brackets following the name Pelzer used.

did.langmaterial: In English, Dutch, Indonesian, and German.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Folder titles are the ones Pelzer gave to his original files, and often the names of the geographic locations he references, usually colonist in origin, are no longer recognized. In the case of this subseries, Pelzer frequently used the term "Malaya", which refers to a number of historical and current political entities related to what is currently Peninsular Malaysia.

did.unittitle: Chan, K.W. -- Economic Aspects of Malayan [Malaysian] Rubber Supply
The Contrast of Land Alienation in Trengganu and Johore by Dorothy Guyot. Preliminary draft
Farm Surveys and Farmers' Association Business Service Plan by Koh Theam Hee and Liao Hsing Chia
Farmer's Association: the Development of Decision Making Bodies by Koh Theam Lee
Land Capability Classification Report Johore State
Malaysia Senarai Penerbitan Lengkap Current List of Publications
Negeri Perak Penyata Tahunan 1967 Oleh Dato' Haji Wan Hassan Bin Abdul Halim, DPMK, PMP, KMN, PJK. Pegawai Hutan Negeri
Physiographic Implications of Lateritein Malaya [Malaysia] by R.J. Eyles
The Plantation as the Model for Pioneer Settlement Sponsored by the Malaysian Government -- Karl J. Pelzer
Planters' Bulletin of the Rubber Research Institute of Malaya
The Plight of the Plantation Workers in West Malaysia, with Special Reference to Indian Labour-- Gunawan and Raghavan
The Present Land Use of Malacca by B.D. Donaldson
Progress Report of Development in the State of Perak
Publication lists and order forms -- Malaysia
A Russian in Kelatan? By A.J.S. Reid
A Scheme for Supervised Farm Credit: Preliminary Draft by Koh Theam Hee and Liao Hsing Chia
Chin See-Chung "A Look at the Tropical Rainforest"
Wah, F. Chang Kwong -- rubber in Malaya [Malaysia]

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Folder titles are the ones Pelzer gave to his original files, and often the names of the geographic locations he references, usually colonist in origin, are no longer recognized. In the case of this subseries, Truk Atoll refers to what is now called Chuuk Lagoon, a part of Chuuk State and the larger Caroline Islands group within the Federated States of Micronesia. The Caroline Islands now exist politically as 2 entities: the Republic of Palau and the Federated States of Micronesia. After the Second World War the Islands became trust territories of the United States (hence folders in this subseries that reference the "U.S. Central Carolines"). The Federated States of Micronesia gained independence in 1986, followed by Palau in 1994. In other, less frequent, cases of geographic name changes within this subseries, the current recognized name appears in brackets next to the name Pelzer used.

bioghist.p: Sundaland consists of the islands of Sumatra, Java, and Borneo among the other surrounding islands and the mainland cape on the Asian mainland.

did.unittitle: Journal Über Gebölz Garten Heiden und Weinbaues angefangen im Jahre 1834. Allexanderthal
Series V. Pelzer publications and writings
Die Arbeitswanderungen in Südostasien: Eine wirtschafts- und bevölkerungsgeographische Untersuchung
Discussion of Miss Peter Ady's Paper on Africa's Economic Potential
Geopolitics of the Sumatran Pepper Trade: Competition Between New England Captain-Traders and the British and Dutch East India Companies
Mass Migration and Resettlement Projects in Southeast Asia Since 1945
Massa Migrasi di Asia Selatan Dan Asia Tenggara Sesudah Perang Dunia Kedua
Notes from the Desk of the President of the Association for Asian Studies
Pioneer Settlement in the Asiatic Tropics
Planter and Peasant: Colonial Policy and the Agrarian Struggle in East Sumatra 1863-1947 -- research proposal
Post World War II Mass Migration of South East Asian Refugees
The Impact of Science on Southeast Asia
The Plantation as the Model for Pioneer Settlement Sponsored by the Malaysian Government
The Resource Pattern of Southeast Asia
The Role of the Shifting Cultivator in the Development of Export Agriculture, With Special Reference to Indonesia
The Spanish Tobacco Monopply in the Philippines, 1782-1883, and the Dutch Forced Cultivation System in Indonesia, 1834-1870
The Swidden Cultivator as Producer of Agricultural Exports: an Indonesian Case Study
Western Impact on East Sumatra and North Tapanuli: the Roles of the Planter and the Missionary
Series VI. Photographs, slides, and film
Commercial negatives of Medan with Dutch and English titles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of irrigation project (in Dutch).
